title: Promise
body:
## Definition

A **Promise** is an object representing the eventual result of async work — states: pending, fulfilled, or rejected — with `.then` / `.catch` handlers scheduled as [microtasks](concept:microtask).

## Why it exists

Replaces callback pyramids with composable async chains and standardized error propagation.

## How it works

Settling a Promise queues reactions on the [microtask](concept:microtask) queue. Chaining returns new Promises; returning a Promise from `.then` flattens the chain. Integrates with [async/await](concept:async-await).

## Example

```js
fetch("/api/user")
  .then((res) => res.json())
  .then((user) => console.log(user.name))
  .catch(console.error);
```

## Common confusion

`new Promise` executor runs synchronously; handlers run async. Unhandled rejections crash Node / trigger global handlers.

## In practice

All modern network code, TanStack Query, and React Server Actions build on Promises.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

A **[Promise](concept:promise)** is an object representing the eventual result of [async](concept:async-await) work — states: pending, fulfilled, or rejected — with `.then` / `.catch` handlers scheduled as [microtasks](concept:microtask).

## Why it exists

Replaces callback pyramids with composable [async](concept:async-await) chains and standardized error [propagation](concept:event-bubbling-capturing).

## How it works

Settling a [Promise](concept:promise) queues reactions on the [microtask](concept:microtask) queue. Chaining returns [new](concept:new-operator) [Promises](concept:promise); returning a [Promise](concept:promise) from `.then` flattens the chain. Integrates with [async/await](concept:async-await).

## Example

```js
fetch("/api/user")
  .then((res) => res.json())
  .then((user) => console.log(user.name))
  .catch(console.error);
```

## Common confusion

`new Promise` executor runs synchronously; handlers run [async](concept:async-await). [Unhandled rejections](concept:unhandled-promise-rejection) crash Node / trigger global handlers.

## In practice

All modern network code, [TanStack Query](concept:tanstack-query), and [React Server Actions](concept:react-server-actions) build on [Promises](concept:promise).
